title: Mobs On Demand
description: Create simple attracter items to pull mobs to you! Nice for Farmer RP and rounding up mobs!
body:
![mod](https://i.imgur.com/ighIwNz.png)
Mobs on Demand: Take Control of Your Farm!

Are you tired of running around your Minecraft world trying to herd your farm animals? Do you wish you could gather your livestock with just a click? Look no further than Mobs on Demand!

Mobs on Demand is a game-changing mod that gives you the power to attract specific farm animals directly to you. No more tedious chasing or building elaborate traps; simply use our specially crafted attractors and let the magic happen!

Features:

FTBChunks support 

Unique Attractor Items:

- Chicken Attractor: Cluck your way to success and have all the chickens flocking to you.
- Cow Attractor: Moo-ve those cows without breaking a sweat.
- Sheep Attractor: Gather your woolly friends in no time.
- Pig Attractor: Oink your way to a pig-filled paradise.
- Villager Attractor: Gather villagers to you like the town Crier!

Wide Radius Attraction: Attract animals from a distance of up to 64 blocks. Perfect for large farms and sprawling homesteads.

Easy Crafting Recipes: Craft your attractors using everyday materials like wheat and wool. Keep your farm stocked with these handy tools.

No Configuration Needed: Plug and play! This mod is designed for ease of use, so you can start attracting mobs right away.

How It Works:

Simply craft the desired attractor item and right-click to activate it. The corresponding animals within a 64-block radius will be drawn to you as if you were their favorite treat. Want to lead them to a new pasture or gather them for breeding? Mobs on Demand makes it easy.

Why Choose Mobs on Demand?

With Mobs on Demand, you have the ultimate tool for managing your farm animals. Whether you're a seasoned farmer or just getting started, this mod saves you time and effort, letting you focus on what really matters—expanding your Minecraft empire!
[![](https://i.imgur.com/l815YIN.png)](https://bisecthosting.com/PixelDream)
